name: what-if-oracle
tagline: Runs a structured 4-6 branch what-if scenario analysis (best/likely/worst/wildcard/contrarian/second-order) for decision-making and risk.
verdict: Well-structured reasoning skill with clear phases, concrete templates, and worked examples; no security or file-operation risk since it is purely conversational. Weak spots: no stated exclusions so it could misfire on adjacent tasks, no default for Quick vs Deep mode selection, and its referenced scenario-templates.md file is not verifiable in the evaluated.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / exclusion guidance anywhere; the 'What This Is NOT' section (lines 166-170) covers output epistemics, not scope boundaries.
No default stated for choosing Quick vs Deep Oracle mode (lines 146-152) when the user doesn't specify one; agents could diverge.
references/scenario-templates.md is referenced (lines 26, 176) but not included in the evaluated content; existence unverifiable.
